name: Firecrawl
description: Web scraping and crawling API. Turns any website into LLM-ready markdown, HTML, screenshots, links, or structured JSON. Handles JavaScript rendering, anti-bot bypass, sitemap discovery, web search, and natural-language structured extraction. v2 API.

tools.name: scrape
tools.description: Scrape a single URL and return its content in LLM-ready formats (markdown, HTML, links, screenshots, or structured JSON). Handles JavaScript rendering and anti-bot bypass. Use for one-off page extraction. For multiple URLs, use crawl or batch_scrape.

formats.description: Output formats to return. Default is markdown only.

onlyMainContent.description: Strip headers, navs, footers, and other boilerplate. Default true.

includeTags.description: HTML tags or CSS selectors to include (e.g. ['article', 'main', '.post-body'])

excludeTags.description: HTML tags or CSS selectors to exclude (e.g. ['nav', 'footer', '.ads'])

waitFor.description: Milliseconds to wait for JavaScript to finish rendering before scraping

removeBase64Images.description: Strip base64-encoded images from the markdown output

blockAds.description: Block ads, trackers, and cookie-consent popups

proxy.description: Proxy type. 'enhanced' is best for anti-bot sites; 'auto' picks per-request.

actions.description: Pre-scrape browser actions to perform (click, type, scroll, wait, etc.). See Firecrawl docs for the action schema.

location.description: Geographic location settings (country, languages) for proxy and emulation

maxAge.description: Use cached content if it's no older than N milliseconds. Default 48h.

storeInCache.description: Whether to store this scrape in Firecrawl's cache

jsonOptions.description: When 'json' is in formats, structured-extraction options: { schema, prompt, systemPrompt }

tools.name: crawl
tools.description: Start an asynchronous crawl of an entire website. Returns a job id immediately — poll firecrawl_crawl_status with that id to retrieve scraped pages as they complete. Use for multi-page extraction. For one URL use scrape; for a fixed URL list use batch_scrape.

prompt.description: Natural-language description of what to crawl. Firecrawl uses this to auto-derive include/exclude paths and depth.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

maxDiscoveryDepth.description: Maximum link-discovery depth (independent from page-content depth)

includePaths.description: Regex patterns of URL pathnames to include (e.g. ['/blog/.*', '/docs/.*'])

excludePaths.description: Regex patterns of URL pathnames to exclude (e.g. ['/admin/.*', '/login.*'])

regexOnFullURL.description: Match include/exclude regexes against the full URL instead of just the pathname

ignoreQueryParameters.description: Treat URLs that differ only by query string as the same page

sitemap.description: Sitemap handling: 'skip' ignores it, 'include' uses it as a hint, 'only' restricts crawl to sitemap URLs

crawlEntireDomain.description: Allow following sibling and parent URLs, not just children

allowSubdomains.description: Follow links into subdomains of the starting URL

delay.description: Seconds to wait between scrapes — use this to respect rate limits

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum number of pages scraped in parallel

webhook.description: Webhook config for crawl events: { url, headers, metadata }

scrapeOptions.description: Per-page scrape options applied to every crawled URL — same shape as the scrape tool's body (formats, onlyMainContent, headers, waitFor, etc.)

tools.name: crawl_status
tools.description: Poll a running crawl job by id. Returns counts (total/completed), status (scraping/completed/failed), credit usage, and any pages scraped so far. Call repeatedly until status='completed'.

id.description: The crawl job id returned by firecrawl_crawl

tools.name: crawl_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a running crawl job by id. Useful when a crawl is taking longer than expected or hitting unexpected pages.

id.description: The crawl job id returned by firecrawl_crawl

tools.name: map
tools.description: Discover all URLs on a website without scraping their content. Fast — uses sitemap.xml + light crawling. Use this to plan a targeted scrape, audit site structure, or filter by keyword via 'search'. 1 credit per website.

search.description: Filter/order returned URLs by relevance to this search query (e.g. 'pricing', 'blog')

sitemap.description: Sitemap handling: 'skip' bypasses it, 'include' uses it + crawls, 'only' uses sitemap exclusively

ignoreQueryParameters.description: Drop query parameters from returned URLs

ignoreCache.description: Bypass Firecrawl's sitemap cache and re-fetch fresh URLs

location.description: Geographic location settings (country, languages) for proxy and emulation

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the web and optionally scrape result pages in one call. Perfect for research, competitive analysis, lead generation, and SEO. Set scrapeOptions to also receive the full content of each result.

limit.description: Number of results to return (1 credit per result if scrapeOptions is set)

sources.description: Search sources to query. Default is web.

tbs.description: Time-based search filter (Google syntax: 'qdr:d' = past day, 'qdr:w' = past week, 'qdr:m' = past month, 'qdr:y' = past year)

country.description: ISO country code for localized results (e.g. 'US', 'GB', 'DE')

location.description: Specific location string for local search results (e.g. 'San Francisco, California, United States')

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: Skip results with invalid URLs instead of erroring

scrapeOptions.description: When set, scrape each search result and return its content. Same shape as the scrape tool's body.

tools.name: extract
tools.description: Extract structured JSON from one or more URLs using natural language and/or a JSON schema. Returns a job id — poll firecrawl_extract_status to retrieve results. Use this when you want typed data instead of raw markdown.

urls.description: URLs to extract from. Glob patterns supported (e.g. 'https://example.com/blog/*').

prompt.description: Natural-language description of what to extract. Used alongside 'schema' to guide extraction.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the structure of the desired output

enableWebSearch.description: Allow Firecrawl to perform supplementary web searches if the target pages don't contain enough info

ignoreSitemap.description: Bypass sitemap.xml when scanning the target sites

includeSubdomains.description: Extend extraction to subdomains of the provided URLs

showSources.description: Include the source URLs that contributed to each extracted field in the response

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: Skip invalid URLs and continue with the rest

scrapeOptions.description: Per-page scrape options applied while extracting

tools.name: extract_status
tools.description: Poll an extract job by id. Returns the structured data once status='completed'.

id.description: The extract job id returned by firecrawl_extract

tools.name: batch_scrape
tools.description: Scrape a fixed list of URLs in one job. Returns a job id — poll firecrawl_batch_scrape_status to retrieve results. Use this when you already have the list of URLs (vs crawl, which discovers them).

formats.description: Output formats applied to every URL

onlyMainContent.description: Strip headers, navs, footers, and other boilerplate

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: Skip invalid URLs instead of failing the whole batch

webhook.description: Webhook config for batch events: { url, headers, metadata }

tools.name: batch_scrape_status
tools.description: Poll a batch scrape job by id. Returns counts and any scraped pages so far.

id.description: The batch scrape job id returned by firecrawl_batch_scrape

tools.name: batch_scrape_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a running batch scrape job.

id.description: The batch scrape job id returned by firecrawl_batch_scrape

tools.name: crawl_errors
tools.description: Retrieve per-page errors for a crawl job. Use after crawl_status reports failures or when total > completed, to see exactly which URLs failed and why.

id.description: The crawl job id returned by firecrawl_crawl

tools.name: batch_scrape_errors
tools.description: Retrieve per-URL errors for a batch scrape job. Use after batch_scrape_status reports failures.

id.description: The batch scrape job id returned by firecrawl_batch_scrape

tools.name: crawl_active
tools.description: List all crawl jobs currently running for this account. No arguments. Useful for monitoring without needing to track job ids yourself.

tools.name: crawl_params_preview
tools.description: Preview what a crawl request would do — Firecrawl resolves the prompt/include/exclude/sitemap config into the actual normalized parameters and an estimated page count without spending credits. Use before launching expensive crawls.

prompt.description: Natural-language description of what to crawl
